zh: 这是最后的另一个数据点，你有一堆关于如何处理所有这些信息的建议，这与你稍后推荐的内容有关。

speaker: Unknown
en: ... what to do with all this information, and that connects to something that you recommend later.
zh: ……如何处理所有这些信息，这与你稍后推荐的内容有关。

speaker: Unknown
en: But before we get there, one of the other really interesting takeaways was around layoffs and how layoffs impact fear of layoffs impact burnout.
zh: 但在我们要谈到那之前，另一个真正有趣的要点是关于裁员以及裁员如何影响对裁员的恐惧进而影响职业倦怠。

speaker: Unknown
en: Talk about that.
zh: 谈谈那个吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: We did ask people how worried they are about being laid off.
zh: 我们确实问过人们，他们对自己被裁员有多担心。

speaker: Unknown
en: And as you can see, 72% of our sample are worried to a certain extent about being laid off, and 41.2% are at least moderately worried about being laid off.
zh: 正如你所见，72% 的样本在某种程度上担心被裁员，41.2% 的人至少中度担心被裁员。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it is clear that people are feeling that they might be cutting the branch that they're sitting on when it comes to using AI.
zh: 所以很明显，人们感觉到在使用 AI 时，他们可能正在砍断自己所坐的树枝。

speaker: Unknown
en: People are reporting that their company's going all in on agents, all in on these technologies, and it does feel like they may be pushed out at some point, so the worry level is high.
zh: 人们报告说，他们的公司正在全力投入智能体，全力投入这些技术，这确实让人感觉到他们可能在某个时刻被挤出局，所以担忧程度很高。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think if you're watching this, you're starting to notice that there's a ton of ambivalence right now in the community, and a ton of clashes between emotions, feeling energized and enjoying my job, and at the same time, not being very optimistic about it and feeling tired from it and feeling worried that all of that enjoyment is going to end one day when the company I work for decides that I'm no longer needed.
zh: 我认为如果你正在看这个，你开始注意到社区现在存在大量的矛盾情绪，以及情感之间的巨大冲突，感到充满活力并享受我的工作，同时，对此并不乐观，并感到厌倦，担心有一天当我工作的公司决定不再需要我时，所有的享受都会结束。

speaker: Unknown
en: I feel a lot of people listening are probably feeling all this and feeling heard.
zh: 我感觉很多听众可能都在感受这一切，并感到被听到了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So what's interesting is so far the two takeaways.
zh: 有意思的是，到目前为止有两个要点。

speaker: Unknown
en: One is this bifurcation that's happening, and these archetypes that break that group up a little bit further.
zh: 一个是正在发生的这种分化，以及那些将群体进一步细分的行为原型。

speaker: Unknown
en: At the same time, just broadly, burnout is increasing significantly.
zh: 同时，广泛地说，职业倦怠正在显著增加。

speaker: Unknown
en: Optimism is declining.
zh: 乐观情绪正在下降。

speaker: Unknown
en: Talk about just those two, how you think about those two side by side, this idea of many people are much happier and having the best time of their life.
zh: 就谈谈这两个方面，你怎么看待这两个方面并排存在，这个想法是许多人更快乐，过着他们生活中最好的时光。

speaker: Unknown
en: My people are having a really bad time, but then broadly, this optimism and burnout shift.
zh: 我的人过得很糟糕，但从广义上讲，这种乐观和职业倦怠的转变。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think there's something addictive about this technology, and I'll speak for myself for a second and say that I've also never had as much fun in my career as when using these technologies, and I've been using it for too many hours a day playing about and building.
zh: 我认为这项技术有某种成瘾性，我想代表我自己说一秒钟，我也从未在我的职业生涯中像使用这些技术时那样快乐，我每天花了太多小时玩耍和构建。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think many of us, even those who aren't as energized about the technology, we still feel like we're in this fascinating technological playground, and it's very, very hard to disengage from that technology and touch grass, read a book, whatever you love to do in your spare time as well, Lenny.
zh: 我想我们许多人，甚至那些对技术没那么有热情的人，仍然感觉我们处在这个迷人的技术游乐场里，而且很难脱离这种技术去接触现实，读一本书，或者无论你在业余时间喜欢做什么，Lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, yes, we're enjoying using this technology because it's opened up avenues that we've never had, but on the other hand, it's tiring.
zh: 所以是的，我们享受使用这种技术，因为它开辟了我们以前从未有过的途径，但另一方面，它很累人。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's tiring to be constantly learning this technology, and constantly trying to apply it in different contexts, and constantly try to take on more than what your traditional role dictated.
zh: 不断学习这种技术，不断尝试在不同的环境中应用它，并不断尝试承担比你传统角色所规定的更多的事情，这是很累人的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because, again, there aren't any traditional roles anymore, we're just all builders these days, whether that's an official thing within a company or not.
zh: 因为，再说一次，不再有任何传统的角色了，我们这些人现在都只是构建者，无论这是否是公司内部的官方说法。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think that naturally this leads to these clashing emotions, these exciting feelings around what's possible, and how fun it is to use AI.
zh: 所以我认为这自然会导致这些冲突的情绪，这些围绕着可能性和使用 AI 有多么有趣的兴奋感。

speaker: Unknown
en: And on the other hand, the realization that the reason this technology is so exciting is because it's so powerful, and because it's so powerful, it might be coming for us, and to take our jobs, right?
zh: 另一方面，这种意识的出现是因为这项技术之所以如此令人兴奋，是因为它是如此强大，而且因为它是如此强大，它可能会冲着我们而来，并取代我们的工作，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Did you grow up on The Terminator movies?
zh: 你是看着《终结者》系列电影长大的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I keep thinking about The Terminator movies with time-
zh: 我一直在想《终结者》系列电影关于时间——

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know about growing up, but yes, I watched them early in my life.
zh: 我不知道是不是关于长大，但是的，我在我生命早期看过它们。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh my gosh, I love those movies, and I just can't help but wonder when the robots are coming for us.
zh: 天哪，我喜欢那些电影，我就是忍不住想知道机器人什么时候会来找我们要取代我们。

speaker: Unknown
en: So incredible technology might be coming for us at some point.
zh: 所以令人难以置信的技术可能会在某个时刻来找我们要取代我们。

speaker: Unknown
en: Skynet is being built right now.
zh: 天网现在正在被构建。

speaker: Unknown
en: Skynet is coming.
zh: 天网要来了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So there's going to be a really great takeaway around this before we get to that one actually.
zh: 所以在我们讨论那个要点之前，会有一个非常好的要点。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like every single one is so interesting.
zh: 这就像每一个要点都如此有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm just like, "Man, this one's interesting, but they're all interesting."
zh: 我就像，“伙计，这个很有趣，但它们都很有趣。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's get to the next takeaway around people's sense of their own career, and whether they recommend it.
zh: 让我们来看下一个关于人们对自身职业的看法以及他们是否推荐这一行的要点。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is a wild one for me because at least within the research community, I am known as the net promoter score hater.
zh: 这对我来说是一个非常疯狂的要点，因为至少在研究社区里，我是被称为净推荐值（NPS）仇恨者的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: I even built a website dedicated to fighting NPS, npsistheworst.com.
zh: 我甚至建立了一个专门对抗 NPS 的网站，npsistheworst.com。

speaker: Unknown
en: That an actual website?
zh: 那是一个真实的网站吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's an actual website.
zh: 是一个真实的网站。

speaker: Unknown
en: Fun fact, by the way, I dislike NPS so much that I even brought to the domain npsisthebest.com, and I forward people from that to npsistheworst.com.
zh: 顺便说个趣事，我非常不喜欢 NPS，以至于我甚至购买了 npsisthebest.com 这个域名，我把从那里访问的人转发到 npsistheworst.com。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's how committed I am to fighting NPS.
zh: 我对对抗 NPS 就是这样执着。

speaker: Unknown
en: Amazing.
zh: 太棒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: But you and I were both genuinely curious about whether people who are already a few years in and they may be senior or above senior in their roles, what would they say to the person entering the industry now?
zh: 但你我都真的很好奇，那些已经入行几年的人，他们可能是资深或高于资深职位，他们会对接现在进入行业的人说什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Or what would they say to a person considering entering the industry now?
zh: 或者他们会对接考虑现在进入行业的人说什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Would they recommend their role and the tech industry more broadly to a friend, or a relative of theirs?
zh: 他们会向他们的朋友或亲戚推荐他们的职位以及更广泛的科技行业吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And the answer was shocking.
zh: 答案令人震惊。

speaker: Unknown
en: Absolutely shocking.
zh: 绝对令人震惊。

speaker: Unknown
en: So let me show you the data around this, and let me preface this with a quick explanation on NPS because it's not so easy to understand.
zh: 所以让我给你看一下关于这方面的数据，让我先简单解释一下 NPS，因为它不是那么容易理解。

speaker: Unknown
en: NPS is this question around whether you would recommend a product, or in this case your role to a friend or family member, right?
zh: NPS 是关于你是否会推荐一个产品，或者在这种情况下推荐你的职位给朋友或家人的问题，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: And the scale goes from zero to 10, 10 being you would absolutely recommend your role, and zero being you would absolutely not recommend your role.
zh: 评分范围从 0 到 10，10 代表你会绝对推荐你的职位，0 代表你会绝对不推荐你的职位。

speaker: Unknown
en: But then NPS as a whole, when it's calculated, goes from minus 100, which is absolutely terrible, to plus 100.
zh: 但是 NPS 作为一个整体，当它被计算时，范围从负 100 到正 100，负 100 代表绝对糟糕，正 100 代表绝对出色。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the zero point represents neutrality, it represents that the sample as a whole is neutral.
zh: 零点代表中立，它代表样本整体是中立的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not a promoter to use NPS speak, meaning I would promote my role to other people.
zh: 它不是推荐者，用 NPS 的术语来说，这意味着我会向其他人推广我的职位。

speaker: Unknown
en: I would recommend it to other people.
zh: 我会向其他人推荐它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And they're not detractors either, which means I'm not negative about my role, so that's the zero point.
zh: 他们也不是贬损者，这意味着我对我的职位不持负面态度，所以这就是零点。

speaker: Unknown
en: What you are seeing here is that no one is a promoter of their role in tech right now, not even founders who are by far the happiest, happy-go-lucky people in tech right now.
zh: 你在这里看到的是，目前没有人是自己科技职位的推荐者，甚至是创始人，他们目前是科技界迄今为止最快乐、最无忧无虑的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Founders would not recommend their roles.
zh: 创始人不会推荐他们的职位。

speaker: Unknown
en: Neither would people in sales or go-to market, PMs, operations, engineering.
zh: 销售或市场人员、产品经理（PM）、运营、工程人员也不会。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the worst of all, designers and researchers, my community are the least likely to recommend their role to other people coming into tech.
zh: 最糟糕的是设计师和研究人员，我的社区是最不可能向进入科技界的人推荐他们的职位。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's sort of like saying, "I'm kind of swimming in this pool.
zh: 这有点像说，“我有点在这个池子里游泳。

speaker: Unknown
en: The water's kind of okay, but you shouldn't come into these tech waters, they ain't for you."
zh: 水还可以，但你不应该进入这些科技水域，它们不适合你。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh my God, what an interesting takeaway and part of this research.
zh: 天哪，这项研究的一个多么有趣的发现和部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I'm just looking at this chart just as people are seeing this.
zh: 所以我正在看这张图，就像人们正在看到它一样。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone's like, "Nope, don't do what I do."
zh: 每个人都在说，“不，别做我做的工作。”

speaker: Unknown
en: It's interesting.
zh: 很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're going to talk about how founders are the happiest people in tech, but still it's a very stressful, hard job.
zh: 我们将要谈论创始人是科技界最快乐的人，但它仍然是一个非常压力、艰难的工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm surprised it's the least.
zh: 我惊讶它是最低的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You would think many founders would be like, "This is the craziest, gnarliest job.
zh: 你会认为许多创始人会说，“这是最疯狂的、最棘手的工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: You should not do this, it sucks."
zh: 你不应该这样做，这很糟糕。

speaker: Unknown
en: But it's interesting they're the least likely to recommend.
zh: 但有趣的是，他们最不推荐。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a strong narrative right now that there's never been a better time to be a founder.
zh: 目前有一种强烈的说法，现在是从业者的最佳时机。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're a lot less reliant on other people to build.
zh: 你对他人的依赖较少，可以独自构建。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a ton of conversations around the solopreneur or the duopreneur.
zh: 有很多关于 solo-preneur 或 duo-preneur 的讨论。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a ton of conversations around how this is the era to be a founder.
zh: 有很多关于这是成为创始人的时代的讨论。

speaker: Unknown
en: And yet people who are currently in that role aren't very excited about recommending it to other people.
zh: 然而，目前担任这一角色的人并不热衷于向其他人推荐它。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's also another layer to this, which is as you go down in seniority, you are less likely to recommend your role.
zh: 这还有另一层含义，随着资历的降低，你推荐自己角色的可能性也会降低。

speaker: Unknown
en: So execs, VPs, maybe even directors are more likely to recommend their role than people who are ICs.
zh: 所以高管、副总裁，甚至总监比 IC 更有可能推荐他们的角色。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's an interesting one, I've seen all sorts of explanations for that.
zh: 这很有趣，我看过各种解释。

speaker: Unknown
en: One explanation, for example, is that at the top of the pyramid, VPs are benefiting more from AI because they're getting all of this stream of information and knowledge being processed by AI, making their jobs a lot easier, whereas ICs are each scrambling to build all of these micro SaaS products within the companies they work for.
zh: 例如，一种解释是，在金字塔顶端，VP 从 AI 中获益更多，因为他们获得了所有由 AI 处理的信息和知识流，这让他们的工作变得容易得多，而 IC 每个人都在争先恐后地在他们工作的公司内部构建所有这些微型 SaaS 产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: No one knows what anyone else is doing.
zh: 没有人知道别人在做什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a lot of duplicative work.
zh: 有很多重复的工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a lot easier to build products these days, but a lot harder to maintain them.
zh: 这些天构建产品更容易了，但维护它们却难得多。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think a lot of ICs are feeling like, there's a saying, full gas on neutral.
zh: 所以我认为很多 IC 有这种感觉，俗话说，空挡轰油门。

speaker: Unknown
en: You are pushing the pedal to the metal in your car, but you're in neutral gear, you're not going anywhere.
zh: 你在车里把油门踩到底，但你处于空挡，你哪儿也去不了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that's the feeling that a lot of ICs are having these days with these technologies.
zh: 我认为这就是如今很多 IC 对这些技术的感觉。

speaker: Unknown
en: I keep building these things, but I'm not having the impact I want.
zh: 我一直在构建这些东西，但我没有获得我想要的影响力。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone else is building too.
zh: 其他人也在构建。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's lots of confusion, and it's just not worth it anymore.
zh: 有很多困惑，这已经不值得了。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's this interesting also trend, there's this whole meme of don't become part of the permanent underclass.
zh: 这也有一个有趣的趋势，这有一个关于不要成为永久性底层阶级的梗。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I feel like there's a few of these threads throughout this.
zh: 我觉得这里面有几条这样的线索。

speaker: Unknown
en: And here it's like, "Okay, I'm a PM, but it's too late.
zh: 在这里就像，“好吧，我是个 PM，但这太晚了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Don't do this.
zh: 别做这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: The door is closing for this role.
zh: 这个角色的机会之窗正在关闭。

speaker: Unknown
en: Things are changing."
zh: 事情正在发生变化。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's not just PM, it's every role.
zh: 不仅仅是 PM，每个角色都是如此。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone's just like, "Maybe should do something else."
zh: 每个人都只是像，“也许该做点别的。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, I would agree with that, and I think it's unfortunate.
zh: 是的，我同意，我认为这很不幸。

speaker: Unknown
en: I feel like in the past, people were pushed to CS to learn to code.
zh: 我觉得在过去，人们被推动去学 CS 和写代码。

speaker: Unknown
en: I know that these days, for myself as a parent, I don't know what to tell my kids to do anymore.
zh: 我知道如今，作为父母，我不知道该告诉我的孩子做什么了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm not sure what roles will be there for them in just a few years.
zh: 我不确定几年后还会有什么角色给他们。

speaker: Unknown
en: These are very confusing times, and I think tech employees are feeling that as well.
zh: 这是非常令人困惑的时期，我认为科技员工也感受到了这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: They don't understand what's in line for them in the future, so we certainly don't know to recommend to others like, "Yeah, in five years, it's going to be great."
zh: 他们不理解未来为他们准备了什么，所以我们当然不知道如何向其他人推荐，“是的，五年后，这会很棒。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Get into plastics.
zh: 加入塑料行业。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like no one knows.
zh: 就像没人知道一样。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just zoom out for a moment.
zh: 只是暂时拉远视角看一下。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's so insane how much change we're living through right now.
zh: 我们现在正在经历的变化如此巨大，这太疯狂了。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is not a result we would've seen a year or two ago.
zh: 这是我们一两年前不会看到的结果。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is so unstable and uncertain, and this is very new for people in tech, I think.
zh: 这是如此不稳定和不确定，我认为这对于科技行业的人来说是非常新的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I like to use certain metaphors or examples from other areas in my life to explain these things, so I believe you spoke in the past to Scott Wu, the Co-Founder and CEO of Cognition.
zh: 我喜欢用我生活中的某些比喻或例子来解释这些事情，我相信你过去曾与 Cognition 的联合创始人兼 CEO Scott Wu 谈过。

speaker: Unknown
en: Devin.
zh: Devin。

speaker: Unknown
en: And they are the makers of Devin.
zh: 他们是 Devin 的开发者。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I recall that when he described the product, he talked about this ladder that the product progressed on.
zh: 我记得当他描述这个产品时，他谈到了产品在这个阶梯上发展的过程。

speaker: Unknown
en: It went from a high school CS student to a college intern to a junior engineer, and at this point probably a senior or a staff level engineer.
zh: 它从一名高中 CS 学生到一名大学实习生，再到一名初级工程师，此时可能是一名资深或首席工程师。

speaker: Unknown
en: I would say that we are all watching this technology climb the rungs of this ladder and advance, and we feel like the technology is pulling those rungs from under us.
zh: 我想说我们都在看着这项技术攀登这个阶梯的横档并前进，我们感觉技术正在把我们脚下的横档抽走。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so the further we've gone up the ladder, the better we feel about our careers and the more stability we have.
zh: 因此，我们在阶梯上攀登得越高，我们就对自己的职业生涯感觉越好，我们也拥有更多的稳定性。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the lower we are on that ladder, those rungs getting pulled under our feet.
zh: 但我们在那个阶梯上越低，那些横档就会被从我们脚下抽走。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so it makes lots of sense that we wouldn't recommend being on that ladder to anyone else because the rungs are disappearing.
zh: 所以我们要不向其他人推荐待在那个阶梯上是非常合理的，因为那些横档正在消失。

speaker: Unknown
en: Man, there's so much to talk about with all this, but let me point out a couple things as I look at this and then we can move on to the next takeaway.
zh: 哎，关于这一大堆要说的太多了，但在看这个的时候让我指出几件事，然后我们可以继续下一个要点。

speaker: Unknown
en: One is just, it's interesting that sales and PM are the least bad at don't get into this role.
zh: 第一件只是，有趣的是销售和 PM 是最不坏的，也就是不让大家进入这个角色。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's interesting.
zh: 这很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: I wouldn't have expected that.
zh: 我没想到会这样。

speaker: Unknown
en: Salespeople not as scared about the future of their role.
zh: 销售人员对他们角色的未来并不那么害怕。

speaker: Unknown
en: PM.
zh: PM。

speaker: Unknown
en: Interesting.
zh: 有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's a big audience here.
zh: 这里有很大的受众群体。

speaker: Unknown
en: Not as scared.
zh: 并不那么害怕。

speaker: Unknown
en: As you said, research, design, the most don't do this.
zh: 正如你所说，研究、设计，最不让大家做这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: As a researcher, you touched on this a little bit, but I'm just curious, how does it feel seeing this result?
zh: 作为一个研究员，你稍微触及了这一点，但我只是好奇，看到这个结果感觉如何？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's tough.
zh: 很难受。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think the research community has lived with a lot of concerns and trauma and anxiety over the years around having a seat at the table, around finding meaning in our work, around topics like the democratization of research and other people taking on our roles and eventually replacing us.
zh: 我认为研究界多年来一直带着很多担忧、创伤和焦虑生活，关于在桌边有一席之地，关于在我们的工作中寻找意义，关于像研究民主化这样的主题以及其他人承担我们的角色并最终取代我们。

speaker: Unknown
en: It definitely hurts a lot to see this.
zh: 看到这一点的确非常伤人。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think there's a very important role that research needs to play in the future of building products.
zh: 我认为研究在构建产品的未来中需要扮演非常重要的角色。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that as this technology progressive, we should become even more thoughtful about what we build rather than less thoughtful.
zh: 我认为随着这项技术的进步，我们应该对我们构建的东西更加深思熟虑，而不是不那么深思熟虑。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've heard the narratives around it's so cheap to build, just build a thousand prototypes and see what works.
zh: 我听说过这样的说法，现在构建太便宜了，只需构建一千个原型来看看什么有效。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think you've heard this in a recent conversation as well.
zh: 我想你也最近在一次谈话中听到过这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: I would suggest that we're just going to get even more burnt out that way, I would not recommend that approach personally.
zh: 我想建议的是，那样做只会让我们更加精疲力尽，我个人不会推荐那种方法。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think research has a very, and design, has a very important part to play in raising the ceiling of what's possible.
zh: 我认为研究，以及设计，在提高可能性的上限方面发挥着非常重要的作用。

speaker: Unknown
en: Whereas AI has mostly lowered the bar, but it hasn't raised the ceiling.
zh: 而 AI 大多降低了门槛，但它并没有提高上限。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I can also tell you that PMs being somewhat more likely to recommend their roles, even though they're not likely to recommend their roles, does actually make sense to me.
zh: 我还可以告诉你，PM 稍微更有可能推荐他们的角色，即使他们不太可能推荐他们的角色，这对我来说确实是有道理的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's more of a generalist role, I would claim, than some of these other roles.
zh: 我会声称，这比其他一些角色更像是一个通才角色。

speaker: Unknown
en: And this is the era of the generalist, if you would ask me, the AI era.
zh: 如果你问我的话，这是通才的时代，AI 时代。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm the most surprised by sales and go-to-market because if we are indeed in some sort of SaaSpocalypse, which I don't personally believe we are, but it's been said, then I don't think sales and go-to-market people should be feeling too great about recommending their roles.
zh: 我最惊讶的是销售和进入市场，因为如果我们确实处于某种 SaaSpocalypse 之中，我个人不认为我们是，但这已经说过了，那么我不认为销售和进入市场的人应该对推荐他们的角色感觉太好。

speaker: Unknown
en: But maybe they believe that sales at this point cannot be done by AI.
zh: 但也许他们相信在这个阶段销售不能由 AI 完成。

speaker: Lenny
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Noam Segal
en: Just that.
zh: 就是这样。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's just so much I want to talk about here.
zh: 关于这里我想谈的太多了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's important to note, this is a very important note that I'm going to say here, this doesn't mean design is going away or research is going away.
zh: 需要注意的是，这是我在这里要说的一个非常重要的注记，这并不意味着设计正在消失或研究正在消失。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is just saying people in that role right now, the narrative in their head is don't do this.
zh: 这只是说现在在这个角色中的人，他们脑海中的叙事是不要做这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: Does not mean this is over, because it could just be all this hype that we keep hearing.
zh: 并不意味着这就结束了，因为这可能只是我们一直听到的所有炒作。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm guilty of adding to it from various guests, but this is just how people feel, doesn't mean this is what's happening.
zh: 我对通过各种客人增加这一点感到内疚，但这只是人们的感觉，并不意味着这就是正在发生的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: This entire unique project in tech is all about how people feel.
zh: 科技界这个独特的项目完全是关于人们的感觉。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not about the realities of working in tech.
zh: 这不是关于在科技界工作的现实。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's about people's emotions, about how people feel.
zh: 它是关于人们的情绪，关于人们的感觉。

speaker: Unknown
en: I really do think no one other than us has really captured these emotions in the way that we have.
zh: 我真的认为，除了我们之外，没有其他人真正以我们的方式捕捉到了这些情绪。

speaker: Unknown
en: But yeah, that's a great reminder.
zh: 但是是的，那是一个很好的提醒。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is not an image of reality.
zh: 这不是现实的图像。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is an image of how people are feeling in tech right now.
zh: 这是人们对科技界现在的感觉的图像。

speaker: Unknown
en: Another quick data point that we want to make sure to communicate here, you touched on this, but I don't know if you actually made this as clear to people as it should be.
zh: 我们要确保在这里传达的另一个快速数据点，你触及了这一点，但我不知道你是否真正让人们像应该的那样清楚这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: So this is looking at, would you recommend people get into my role?
zh: 所以这是在观察，你会推荐人们进入我的角色吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: We also asked just how are you feeling about your role?
zh: 我们还询问了你对你角色的感觉如何？

speaker: Unknown
en: Are you happy being in this role?
zh: 你在这个角色中快乐吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And the result there was, I'm actually doing okay, I'm happy.
zh: 那里的结果是，我其实做得还好，我很快乐。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm happy a designer, researcher.
zh: 我很快乐，作为一个设计师、研究员。

speaker: Unknown
en: So as you said, it's this kind of, the water's fine, but don't get in.
zh: 所以正如你所说，这是这种，水很好，但别进来。

speaker: Unknown
en: Talk a little bit about that just to make sure that point is clear.
zh: 稍微谈谈这一点，以确保这一点清楚。

speaker: Unknown
en: When we asked people things like, are they enjoying their job?
zh: 当我们问人们像这样的事情时，他们享受他们的工作吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: As we talked about earlier, most people said that overall, yes, they're having fun in their role.
zh: 正如我们之前谈到的，大多数人说，总的来说，是的，他们在他们的角色中玩得开心。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think what's going on here is that again, people are lacking optimism compared to what they were.
zh: 我认为这里发生的情况是，人们相比以前缺乏乐观。

speaker: Unknown
en: Optimism has gone down.
zh: 乐观已经下降。

speaker: Unknown
en: So there's a big difference between how people are feeling now about their role, given what they've achieved in their careers, and where they are on the ladder compared to what they're extrapolating might happen in a year, in three years, in five years.
zh: 所以，人们对他们当前角色的感受，与考虑到他们在职业生涯中取得的成就以及他们在梯子上的位置相比，以及与人们推断一年后、三年后、五年后可能会发生的情况相比，存在巨大差异。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what people are extrapolating might happen is clearly more negative than their current experiences.
zh: 人们推断可能发生的事情明显比他们当前的经历更消极。

speaker: Unknown
en: So yeah, I mean all in all, we're doing okay as an industry.
zh: 是的，我是说总的来说，作为一个行业我们做得还不错。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're enjoying, we're trying new things, we're experiencing a complete paradigm shift in how we work.
zh: 我们很享受，我们在尝试新事物，我们正在经历工作方式上的完全范式转变。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's fun for a while, but it seems like people feel like this is not going in the best of directions.
zh: 这在一段时间内很有趣，但似乎人们觉得这不是朝着最好的方向发展。

speaker: Unknown
en: And of course I hope we're all wrong.
zh: 当然，我希望我们都错了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I hope we're all wrong.
zh: 我希望我们都错了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Me too.
zh: 我也是。

speaker: Unknown
en: As a natural optimist, I'm like, "It's going to be all right."
zh: 作为一个天生的乐观主义者，我想，「会没事的。」

speaker: Unknown
en: So we'll see.
zh: 我们拭目以待。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm a natural optimist too, and I'm realizing so far we haven't been in too much of optimism mode, so I hope we manage to get to some of that as well.
zh: 我也是一个天生的乐观主义者，我也意识到到目前为止，我们的乐观模式还不够，所以我希望我们也能努力达到那种状态。

speaker: Unknown
en: This episode is brought to you by Mercury, radically different banking loved by over 300,000 entrepreneurs and now with command.
zh: 本集由 Mercury 赞助播出，这是超过 30 万企业家所钟爱的截然不同的银行业务，现在推出了 Command。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've been a customer of Mercury's for over six years.
zh: 我成为 Mercury 的客户已经超过六年了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have never once thought about leaving.
zh: 我从未想过离开。

speaker: Unknown
en: Mercury is basically what happens when banking is built by product people, not by bankers.
zh: Mercury 基本上就是当银行业务是由产品人员而不是银行家构建时会发生的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: They make it so easy.
zh: 它们让它变得如此简单。

speaker: Unknown
en: Dare I say fun to send invoices, move money around, set up virtual cards for folks on my team.
zh: 我敢说发送发票、调动资金、为我的团队成员设置虚拟卡是有趣的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Does your bank have an API, a terminal native CLI, or an AI-ready MCP server?
zh: 你的银行有 API、终端原生 CLI 或就绪的 AI MCP 服务器吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't think so.
zh: 我不这么认为。

speaker: Unknown
en: And just recently, they launched Command, a conversational interface built directly into Mercury, which acts as your financial operator.
zh: 就在最近，它们推出了 Command，这是一个直接构建在 Mercury 中的对话式界面，充当您的财务操作员。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've been using Command to transfer money around to figure out what categories I've been spending the most money in, analyze my cash flows.
zh: 我一直在使用 Command 来调动资金，以弄清楚我在哪些类别上花钱最多，分析我的现金流。

speaker: Unknown
en: And just today, I used it to find out how much I've made from a specific sponsor over the past year.
zh: 就在今天，我用它来找出我在过去一年中从某个特定赞助商那里赚了多少钱。

speaker: Unknown
en: I just asked how much have I made from X over the past year?
zh: 我只是问，我在过去一年中从 X 那里赚了多少钱？

speaker: Unknown
en: 10 seconds later, I have an answer.
zh: 10 秒钟后，我就得到了答案。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is so freaking cool.
zh: 这真是太酷了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Visit mercury.com to learn more and apply online in minutes.
zh: 访问 mercury.com 了解更多信息并在几分钟内在线申请。

speaker: Unknown
en: Mercury is a FinTech company, not an FDIC-insured bank.
zh: Mercury 是一家金融技术公司，而不是 FDIC 承保的银行。

speaker: Unknown
en: Banking services provided through Choice Financial Group and Column N.A., Members FDIC.
zh: 银行业务由 Choice Financial Group 和 Column N.A. 提供，均为 FDIC 成员。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's talk about takeaway four.
zh: 让我们来谈谈第四个要点。

speaker: Unknown
en: Looked at productivity, looked at quality, look at just the upside of AI.
zh: 看了看生产力，看了看质量，看看 AI 的上行空间。

speaker: Unknown
en: This was a very interesting one to me.
zh: 对我来说这是一个非常有趣的发现。

speaker: Unknown
en: In last year's survey that we did on the impact of AI specifically on productivity and quality, people were rather bullish about the technology and how much better it makes them, not just more efficient, but better.
zh: 在去年我们针对 AI 对生产力和质量的具体影响所做的调查中，人们对这项技术以及它让他们变得多么好持相当看涨的态度，不仅是更高效，而且是更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: And this time around, we saw some interesting findings.
zh: 而这一次，我们看到了一些有趣的发现。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let me start with this.
zh: 让我先从这一点开始。

speaker: Unknown
en: And this is the initial view, but it leads to something very different than I thought would happen.
zh: 这是初步的看法，但它引向了与我预期截然不同的结果。

speaker: Unknown
en: When we simply asked the question, how much better are you at your job thanks to AI?
zh: 当我们简单地问这个问题，AI 让你的工作变好了多少？

speaker: Unknown
en: Unequivocally, the answer was yes.
zh: 毫无疑问，答案是肯定的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can see here that 97.2% of people are saying that AI is making them better at their job, and close to 50% of people are saying that AI is making them very much or extremely better at their job.
zh: 正如你在这里看到的，97.2% 的人说 AI 让他们的工作变得更好，近 50% 的人说 AI 让他们的工作变得非常好或极其好。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's very clear that high level, however you interpret better, which we'll get to in a second, people feel like AI has had an impact.
zh: 所以很清楚，从高层次来看，无论你如何理解更好——我们稍后会谈到——人们觉得 AI 产生了影响。

speaker: Unknown
en: But when you dive deeper into what that means, and you go below the surface level, as you can see here, where doing better actually means something rather different than what you might think.
zh: 但当你深入了解这意味着什么，并超越表面水平，正如你在这里看到的，做得更好实际上意味着一些与你想象的相当不同的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: So when I heard people say they're doing better, I thought they meant that literally, I thought the quality of their work was better, that AI was contributing to better quality of work.
zh: 所以当我听到人们说他们做得更好时，我以为他们的意思是字面意思，我以为他们的工作质量更高了，AI 有助于更好的工作质量。

speaker: Unknown
en: In fact, it's the exact opposite.
zh: 事实上，情况恰恰相反。

speaker: Unknown
en: What we heard from people were two concerning things.
zh: 我们从人们那里听到的是两件令人担忧的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: The first is that I can do more faster, but not better.
zh: 首先是我可以做得更快，但不能做得更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: So again, AI has lowered the floor of what's possible and enabled people to put out a lot more work than they ever have before, whatever that work is, whether it's PRs, PRDs, research projects, prototypes, but the output isn't of higher quality.
zh: 同样，AI 降低了可能性的底线，使人们能够输出比以往任何时候都多的工作，无论是 PR、PRD、研究项目还是原型，但输出质量并没有更高。

speaker: Unknown
en: And perhaps the even more concerning effect, which we heard about, the deeper cost of all of this is the cost of using AI on thinking and judgment.
zh: 也许更令人担忧的影响，也是我们听到的，所有这一切的更深层次的代价是使用 AI 对思考和判断的代价。

speaker: Unknown
en: People essentially told us, "My brain is rotting.
zh: 人们基本上告诉我们，「我的大脑正在腐烂。」

speaker: Unknown
en: My work feels worse."
zh: 我的工作感觉更糟糕了。"

speaker: Unknown
en: And this is related to phenomenon I've heard about recently, is it cognitive rot?
zh: 这与我最近听说的一种现象有关，是不是认知衰退？

speaker: Unknown
en: I think is what I've heard.
zh: 我想我是这么听说的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Basically, this phenomenon where you see the initial output of an AI model and you just accept it.
zh: 基本上，就是这种现象，你看到 AI 模型的初始输出，然后就直接接受了。

speaker: Unknown
en: You don't apply your judgment to it.
zh: 你不对它运用你的判断。

speaker: Unknown
en: You don't apply your thinking to it and you slowly let your mind, your involvement in your work, your agency collapse into this rotting state, so to speak.
zh: 你不对它运用你的思考，你慢慢地让你的心智、你在工作中的参与、你的智能体 崩溃成这种腐烂状态，可以这么说。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, this is such an interesting takeaway.
zh: 是的，这是一个非常有趣的发现。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a positive note to this.
zh: 这其中有一个积极的方面。

speaker: Unknown
en: As you said, I'm looking at the numbers here.
zh: 正如你所说，我在看这里的数据。

speaker: Unknown
en: 82% of people say AI is making them at least moderately better at their job and nearly 50% say very much or extremely better, which may correlate with that original, just this bifurcation.
zh: 82% 的人说 AI 让他们在工作中至少表现得好了一些，近 50% 的人说非常好或极其好，这可能与最初的那个两极分化有关。

speaker: Unknown
en: The people that feel like AI is making them better at their job, probably the people feel most energized.
zh: 那些觉得 AI 让他们在工作中表现更好的人，可能就是那些感觉最充满活力的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: So whether it's true or not, so I think there's a couple happy points.
zh: 所以不管这是否是真的，我认为有几个值得高兴的点。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's one.
zh: 这是其中之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: People feel AI is making them better.
zh: 人们觉得 AI 让他们变得更好了。

speaker: Unknown
en: They feel that.
zh: 他们有这种感觉。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's real even if it may not be.
zh: 所以这是真实的，即使事实可能并非如此。

speaker: Unknown
en: And at the same time, I think they're self-aware the quality is an issue.
zh: 同时，我认为他们自我意识到质量是一个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: And also, maybe I'm atrophying in my ability to say code or write strategy docs.
zh: 而且，也许我在编写代码或撰写战略文档方面的能力正在萎缩。

speaker: Unknown
en: So at least people are self-aware that this is happening, not just I'm killing it, all my stuff is higher quality and it's much better than ever.
zh: 所以至少人们自我意识到这种情况正在发生，而不是觉得“我大杀四方，我所有的东西质量都更高，而且比以往任何时候都好”。

speaker: Unknown
en: They are self-aware, but among other things, I'm a certified coach and I would suggest that beyond being self-aware, we need to act on this.
zh: 他们有自我意识，但除此之外，我还是一名认证教练，我会建议除了自我意识之外，我们需要对此采取行动。

speaker: Unknown
en: There are several explanations I could give to this phenomenon.
zh: 对于这种现象，我可以给出几种解释。

speaker: Unknown
en: One of them might be that the honeymoon period with AI is over.
zh: 其中之一可能是与 AI 的蜜月期已经结束了。

speaker: Unknown
en: We thought it was perfect and the best thing that ever happened to us since sliced bread or whatever typical Americans say, but we've realized it's not quite there yet.
zh: 我们曾经认为它是完美的，是自切片面包以来发生在我们身上最好的事情，或者典型的美国人会说的任何话，但我们意识到它还差得远。

speaker: Unknown
en: And yes, the models are the worst they're ever going to be, they're only getting better, but there's still-
zh: 是的，这些模型正处于它们有史以来最糟糕的阶段，它们只会变得更好，但是仍然——

speaker: Unknown
en: And yes, the models are the worst they're ever going to be, they're only getting better.
zh: 是的，这些模型正处于它们有史以来最糟糕的阶段，它们只会变得更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: But there's still so much they need to get better before we can really say, "Yeah, my work is just so much better and I don't need to be as involved with it."

zh: 所以首先，如果你是一名员工，尤其是如果你是一名在公司工作的 IC（独立贡献者），我们看到的一件事是，那些感到被放大和精力充沛的人报告说，他们实际上深入钻研了特定的任务和需要完成的特定工作，而不是试图做一个包揽万事的通才。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it's the people who try to be or lean too much into being a generalist who end up getting severely burnt out, right?
zh: 我认为那些试图成为或过于倾向于成为通才的人最终会严重耗尽心力，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, I just want to caution people a little bit away from the narratives that you need to be a generalist at this point and just do all of the things and completely ignore the core parts of your role.
zh: 所以，我想稍微告诫人们不要轻信那些关于此时此刻你需要成为一名通才的叙事，不要试图做所有的事情，完全忽略你角色的核心部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that's a bad idea and based on what people are reporting, it's not going to end up with you at least feeling good.
zh: 我认为这是个坏主意，而且根据人们的报告，这最终不会让你感觉良好。

speaker: Unknown
en: I also would love for people to watch out for this squeeze, being overworked for the same pay.
zh: 我也希望大家警惕这种挤压，即同样的薪酬却过度工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: We deserve better.
zh: 我们值得更好的待遇。

speaker: Unknown
en: We've linked in the article to a burnout test.
zh: 我们在文章中链接了一个职业倦怠测试。

speaker: Unknown
en: Take the test, see where you're at on the burnout scale.
zh: 去做个测试，看看你在职业倦怠量表上处于什么位置。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then based on that, reflect on where you are, talk to your manager, scope out your work, again, recalibrate, realign with your manager on your work and make sure that you're not being squeezed out of being positive about your role and being successful in your role.
zh: 然后基于此，反思你目前的处境，与你的经理交谈，界定你的工作范围，再次与你的经理就你的工作进行重新校准和重新对齐，确保你没有因为对角色和成功持积极态度而被挤出。

speaker: Unknown
en: As you're going through this list, let me just make sure people fully understand the frame that we're... Because you made these two buckets basically.
zh: 当你看这个清单时，我想确保人们完全理解我们所使用的框架，因为你基本上把这些内容分成了两桶。

speaker: Unknown
en: Things you can do based on this result.
zh: 基于这个结果你可以做的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: One is just if you're an employee, I think you have five pieces of advice.
zh: 一个是，如果你是一名员工，我认为你有五条建议。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you're a company, you have five-ish pieces of advice.
zh: 如果你是一家公司，你有大约五条建议。

speaker: Unknown
en: So just to be super clear, the first one is just pick a couple of things you want AI to be useful for and just go deep on those couple things, versus trying to spread it all around and AI seems to be doing everything for you.
zh: 所以为了超级清楚，第一点就是选择几件你希望 AI 对你有用的事情，然后就在这几件事情上深入钻研，而不是试图把它到处铺开，让 AI 似乎为你做所有事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Pick a couple things that you're most excited about and then just go really deep there.
zh: 选择几件你最感兴趣的事情，然后就在那里真正做到深入。

speaker: Unknown
en: The other is what you said, just watch the squeeze.
zh: 另一点就是你所说的，警惕这种挤压。

speaker: Unknown
en: Watch you being squeezed and talk to your manager about, "Hey, my scope's increased, nothing changed comp-wise," that kind of thing.
zh: 留意你正受到的挤压，并与你的经理谈谈，“嘿，我的范围扩大了，但在薪酬方面没有任何变化”，诸如此类的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Absolutely.
zh: 没错。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then speak with managers.
zh: 然后要与经理交谈。

speaker: Unknown
en: Again, the third point is that your manager clearly matters more than pretty much anything else when it comes to how you feel about work.
zh: 同样，第三点是，就你对工作的感受而言，你的经理显然比几乎任何其他事情都重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: Protect that relationship, invest in that relationship, build up good communication lines with your manager, learn to manage up better.
zh: 保护这段关系，投资这段关系，与你的经理建立良好的沟通渠道，学会更好地向上管理。

speaker: Unknown
en: That investment in that relationship will pay off greatly in terms of how you feel.
zh: 对这段关系的投资将在你的感受方面带来巨大的回报。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then beyond that, first of all, if you want to try out working for a smaller company or starting your own company, clearly there are some advantages to that, and so it might be worth considering.
zh: 除此之外，首先，如果你想尝试在较小的公司工作或创办自己的公司，显然有一些优势，所以这可能值得考虑。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then, if you're earlier in your career and you feel like the rungs of the ladder are sort of disappearing, and it's becoming harder to climb that ladder, I'm a huge believer in mentorship and the value of mentorship.
zh: 然后，如果你处于职业生涯早期，你觉得梯子的横条正在消失，攀登这个梯子变得越来越难，我是导师制和导师价值的坚定信徒。

speaker: Unknown
en: Strong mentorship is still an incredibly effective thing.
zh: 强有力的导师制仍然是一件非常有效的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Seek the teams, seek the managers, seek the people who are willing to invest in developing you.
zh: 寻找那些团队、寻找那些经理、寻找那些愿意投资培养你的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's incredibly valuable, especially in this era.
zh: 这是非常有价值的，尤其是在这个时代。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, that's what we have for employees.
zh: 所以，这就是我们给员工建议的内容。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then, if you're leading a team, if you're leading a company, then again, as we mentioned before, invest in your managers.
zh: 然后，如果你正在领导一个团队，如果你正在领导一家公司，那么再次，正如我们之前提到的，投资你的经理。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's probably some of the best money you'll ever spend.
zh: 这可能是你花的最值的一笔钱。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's sad to me that only a quarter of our sample, and perhaps tech workers in general, rate their manager as highly effective.
zh: 让我感到难过的是，我们样本中只有四分之一，或许还有科技工作者普遍只有四分之一，认为他们的经理是高效的。

speaker: Unknown
en: That to me is a major, major red flag.
zh: 对我来说，这是一个巨大的、巨大的危险信号。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a major issue in tech.
zh: 这是科技界的一个主要问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, if you want to improve burnout, if you want to elevate enjoyment, if you want to improve retention with these crazy job offers flying around from AI labs, you have to invest in your managers.
zh: 因此，如果你想改善职业倦怠，如果你想提升享受感，如果你想改善留任率，尽管有来自 AI 实验室的那些疯狂的职位邀约满天飞，你必须投资你的经理。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then otherwise, manage that squeeze.
zh: 否则，要管理这种挤压。

speaker: Unknown
en: People are feeling how AI is raising bars in ways that aren't sustainable.
zh: 人们感觉到 AI 正在以一种不可持续的方式提高门槛。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you have to figure out the right level of expectations and the right level of productivity.
zh: 所以你必须找出适当的期望水平和适当的生产力水平。

speaker: Unknown
en: Don't let that bottom rung of the ladder rot.
zh: 不要让梯子的最底层横条腐烂。

speaker: Unknown
en: Make sure that you're doing what you need to for entry level people to advance, because those early career people are also probably some of the most AI native people you can find.
zh: 确保你在为初级人员进步做你需要做的事情，因为那些早期职业生涯的人也可能是一些你能找到的最具 AI 原生特质的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: They find it very natural to use these technologies in some ways more so than people who have been too used to previous paradigms.
zh: 他们发现使用这些技术是很自然的，在某些方面比那些过于习惯旧范式的人更是如此。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then, selfishly, I hope that leaders pay a little bit more attention to design and research and other roles who are feeling more negative than other roles.
zh: 然后，出于私心，我希望领导者多关注一点设计、研究以及其他比其他角色感觉更消极的角色。

speaker: Unknown
en: And keep in mind that this technology, AI, is lifting some people and destabilizing others, and you really have to pay attention to that.
zh: 并且请记住，这项技术，AI，正在提升一些人，同时也让另一些人变得不稳定，你必须真正关注这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: We are clearly not experiencing this technology in the same way.
zh: 我们显然不是以同样的方式体验这项技术。

speaker: Unknown
en: Amazing takeaways.
zh: 令人惊叹的收获。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to double down on that burnout survey that you mentioned.
zh: 我想加倍强调你提到的那个职业倦怠调查。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're going to link to it in the show notes.
zh: 我们将在节目说明中链接到它。

speaker: Unknown
en: But actually people have said it sounds dumb and why am I going to do that?
zh: 但实际上人们说过这听起来很愚蠢，我为什么要那样做？

speaker: Unknown
en: I've definitely gone through periods of burnout and wasn't aware that that was happening.
zh: 我肯定经历过倦怠期，并没有意识到那是正在发生的事。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's so helpful to like, "Oh, wow, this is not normal what I'm feeling right now."
zh: 这很有帮助，就像，“噢，哇，我现在感觉到的这并不是正常的。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And knowing this, that you're okay, you're extremely burnt out right now.
zh: 知道这一点，你是没问题的，你现在极度倦怠。

speaker: Unknown
en: Then you can decide what you want to do with that information.
zh: 然后你可以决定用这些信息做什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: Often, it's talk to a coach, talk to your manager, take some time off if you can But just knowing that is actually really powerful.
zh: 通常，是找教练谈，找经理谈，如果可以的话请一段假，但仅仅是知道这一点实际上真的很强大。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you made this really great quiz based on actual science and research that we'll link to.
zh: 你根据真实的科学和研究做了一个很棒的测验，我们会链接到它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Awesome.
zh: 很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, is there anything else that we haven't talked about?
zh: 现在，还有什么我们没有谈到的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Anything else you want to leave folks with?
zh: 你还有什么想留给听众的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: We've covered a lot, so I don't know if there's anything left.
zh: 我们已经涵盖了很多，所以我不知道还有什么剩下的。

speaker: Unknown
en: We'll obviously link them to the report where they can go deeper.
zh: 我们显然会把他们链接到报告，在那里他们可以更深入地了解。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're also going to do a follow-up based on what comes out of this conversation, what we see in the comments, because this is a lot of information and a lot of scary stuff, some optimistic stuff.
zh: 我们还将根据这次对话的结果和我们在评论中看到的内容做一个后续跟进，因为这是很多信息，很多可怕的东西，也有一些乐观的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're not going to have answers to everything, but we're planning to do a follow-up of just here's what came up.
zh: 我们不会有所有问题的答案，但我们计划做一个后续跟进，这是出现的内容。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's see if we can find any answers.
zh: 让我们看看是否能找到任何答案。

speaker: Unknown
en: But other than that, is there anything else that you want to share?
zh: 但除此之外，还有什么你想分享的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: There's one interesting phenomenon that I wanted to mention.
zh: 有一个有趣的现象我想提一下。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're starting to see this AI guilt, and in particular, amongst people who are early in their career in the sense that people who are early in their career feel like leveraging this technology is a little bit like cheating in a sense.
zh: 我们开始看到这种 AI 内疚感，特别是在那些职业生涯早期的人中，意思是那些职业生涯早期的人觉得利用这项技术有点像作弊。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that guilt declines with seniority.
zh: 这种内疚感随着资历的增加而减少。

speaker: Unknown
en: And in particular, we're seeing people in actually product marketing and data and analytics, which showed up previously in a couple of places, feeling the most guilt.
zh: 特别是，我们看到实际上在产品营销和数据分析领域的人，这出现在之前的几个地方，感到最内疚。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is related to the well-known and well-established imposter phenomenon, "I'm not good enough."
zh: 这与众所周知的冒名顶替现象有关，“我不够好。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Competent people feeling like their success isn't really theirs.
zh: 能干的人觉得他们的成功并不是真正属于他们的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's someone else's.
zh: 是属于别人的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Except this time, we're putting that on AI, on a technology, rather than other people.
zh: 除了这一次，我们把这归咎于 AI，归咎于一项技术，而不是其他人。

speaker: Unknown
en: I would say to all of you who might be feeling that guilt, AI is an incredible technology.
zh: 我想对所有可能感到这种内疚的人说，AI 是一项令人难以置信的技术。

speaker: Unknown
en: Leverage it, learn how to leverage it.
zh: 利用它，学会如何利用它。

speaker: Unknown
en: I doubt it's going away.
zh: 我怀疑它会消失。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's the worst it's ever going to be today.
zh: 它今天是最糟糕的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's only getting better.
zh: 它只会变得更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: So there's no reason to feel guilty about leveraging it, on the contrary.
zh: 所以没有理由因为利用它而感到内疚，恰恰相反。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think inevitably we all are all the time.
zh: 我认为不可避免地，我们所有人一直都在这样做。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, to close [inaudible 01:33:33] I just want to appreciate you.
zh: 好了，为了结束 [听不清 01:33:33] 我只是想感谢你。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's incredible the work that you've done here and the work that we've done together over the many surveys.
zh: 你在这里所做的工作以及我们在许多调查中一起做的工作是令人难以置信的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like a side thing you do with me, running, I don't know, one of the biggest surveys out there around this stuff, maybe the biggest, analyzing it, writing it so beautifully.
zh: 这就像你和我做的一件副业，运行着，我不知道，围绕这些东西的最大调查之一，也许是最大的，分析它，把它写得很漂亮。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think this is a really unique and special report and I appreciate you for doing this with me.
zh: 所以我认为这是一个非常独特和特别的报告，我很感激你和我一起做这件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm really happy about where this all goes and just what we're doing for folks.
zh: 我真的很高兴这一切的发展方向，以及我们为人们所做的一切。

speaker: Unknown
en: Thank you so, so much.
zh: 非常，非常感谢你。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, with that, Noam, where can people find you online if they want to reach out, maybe follow-up questions?
zh: 好了，说到这，Noam，如果人们想联系你，也许有后续问题，他们在哪里可以在线找到你？

speaker: Unknown
en: And how can listeners be useful to you?
zh: 听众怎样才能帮到你？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, so the best places to find me are on LinkedIn.
zh: 是的，所以找到我的最好地方是在 LinkedIn 上。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'd love to connect with anyone who wants to connect on LinkedIn.
zh: 我很乐意与任何想在 LinkedIn 上联系的人建立联系。

speaker: Unknown
en: Or within Lenny's community, within your Slack.
zh: 或者在 Lenny 的社区里，在你的 Slack 里。

speaker: Unknown
en: I hang out there and always happy to talk to people within the community.
zh: 我经常在那里出没，总是很高兴与社区里的人交谈。

speaker: Unknown
en: So please catch me on LinkedIn or on Slack.
zh: 所以请在 LinkedIn 或 Slack 上联系我。

speaker: Unknown
en: That would be best.
zh: 那将是最好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: How can listeners be useful to you, Noam?
zh: 听众怎样才能帮到你，Noam？

speaker: Unknown
en: If you are watching this, what I would like to convey is that I hope this research has been helpful to you.
zh: 如果你正在看这个，我想传达的是，我希望这项研究对你有帮助。

speaker: Unknown
en: And more than anything, I hope that you consider participating in a future research projects of ours.
zh: 最重要的是，我希望你考虑参与我们未来的研究项目。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm really, really glad that we get to do these things, Lenny.
zh: 我真的很高兴我们能做这些事情，Lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I want to give all of you watching the opportunity to learn more about what's going on in tech, how you can be better at your job, how you can feel better about your job and all things in between.
zh: 我想给所有观看的人一个机会，更多地了解科技界正在发生的事情，如何把工作做得更好，如何对工作感觉更好以及介于两者之间的一切。

speaker: Unknown
en: We plan to continue conducting both quick polls of the community, in depth, longer form surveys, interviews, all sorts of things.
zh: 我们计划继续进行针对社区的快速民意调查，深入的、长形式的调查，访谈，各种各样的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so if you get a message, an email, a Slack about a research project, please participate.
zh: 所以如果你收到关于研究项目的消息、电子邮件或 Slack，请参与。

speaker: Unknown
en: Would love to hear your voices, hear your perspectives, and then share that back with the community and hopefully it makes us all better at our jobs, and also feel better about our jobs.
zh: 我们很希望能听到你们的声音，听到你们的观点，然后与社区分享，希望这能让我们都更擅长我们的工作，也让我们对工作感觉更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because I do hope that even though much of this was a little bit tough and perhaps negative, I too, Lenny, am an eternal optimist.
zh: 因为我确实希望，尽管其中很多事情有点艰难，甚至可能是消极的，但我，Lenny，也是一个永恒的乐观主义者。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm a techno optimist.
zh: 我是一个技术乐观主义者。

speaker: Unknown
en: I believe this is going in good directions, and I want to make sure we capture that accurately.
zh: 我相信事情正朝着好的方向发展，我想确保我们要准确地捕捉到这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: So yeah, please participate.
zh: 所以是的，请参与。

speaker: Unknown
en: We'd love to see you in our next research project.
zh: 我们很希望能看到你参与我们的下一个研究项目。

speaker: Unknown
en: Excellent ask.
zh: 很好的请求。

speaker: Unknown
en: With that, Noam, thank you so much for being here.
zh: 话说到这里，Noam，非常感谢你的到来。

speaker: Unknown
en: Thanks a bunch.
zh: 非常感谢。

zh: 非常感谢大家的收听。
